name: Dream Cycle (nightly)
description: Nightly knowledge distillation, fully agent-driven. Step 1 (stage) gathers raw candidates with NO inline LLM and writes two staging files: compile candidates (one extraction prompt each) and taste-eligible transcripts. Steps 2a/2b are parallel agent spawns — a compiler agent extracts wiki entries, a classifier agent reverse-engineers taste signals — so neither half depends on the legacy inline Gemini engine. Step 3 (apply) writes the agents' output, then runs final housekeeping (consolidate / reindex / Codex refresh); it falls back to an inline engine call for anything an agent left unprocessed and runs even if a spawn stalled (onUpstreamFailure=continue). Parameterized by {{wiki_root}} + {{python_path}} so a fresh `digital-me install --runtime dream-cycle` lands the same workflow against any user's wiki + venv.

variables.name: wiki_root
variables.description: Absolute path to the digital-me wiki root (the directory containing wiki/, inbox/, .cache/, config.yaml). digital-me install --runtime dream-cycle supplies this from the --wiki-root flag or $DIGITAL_ME_WIKI_ROOT.

variables.name: python_path
variables.description: Absolute path to a python3 interpreter that has the dream_cycle package installed. digital-me install --runtime dream-cycle auto-supplies the venv's interpreter at ~/.venvs/dream-cycle/bin/python3.

variables.name: classifier_agent_id
variables.description: agentId that the spawn dispatcher uses for the taste-distill step. Should be an agent configured with the openclaw-brain MCP so it can read + write the staging file. Defaults to claude-code; users who route classification through a specialized ops agent (e.g. coo) override here.

variables.name: compile_limit
variables.description: --compile-limit value: cap on wiki entries the compile step extracts in one run. Higher = more coverage per night, more LLM cost.

variables.name: taste_limit
variables.description: --taste-limit value: cap on transcripts the compile step stages for the classifier. Higher = more candidates per night, more classifier cost.

variables.name: recent_days
variables.description: --recent-days value: only inbox files modified within the last N days. 1 = catch up daily; higher = catch up after gaps.

variables.name: taste_staging_path
variables.description: Absolute path to the staging file the stage step writes and the taste-distill + apply steps read. Default lives in /tmp so it doesn't pollute the wiki tree.

variables.name: compile_staging_path
variables.description: Absolute path to the compile staging file the stage step writes (one extraction prompt per candidate, no inline LLM) and the compile-extract + apply steps read. Default lives in /tmp.

variables.name: compiler_agent_id
variables.description: agentId for the compile-extract spawn step. Should be an agent with the openclaw-brain MCP so it can read+write the compile staging file. Defaults to claude-code; this is what makes wiki extraction agent-driven instead of the legacy inline Gemini engine.

steps.name: Compile + stage taste-eligible transcripts
steps.promptTemplate: Run dream_cycle.run with the compile + staging args from this dispatch's command. The script writes wiki entries from inbox/ AND stages taste-eligible transcripts to {{taste_staging_path}}.

steps.name: Compiler agent extracts wiki entries from staged candidates
steps.promptTemplate:
You are the nightly wiki COMPILER. Read the staging file at `{{compile_staging_path}}`.

IMPORTANT — tool policy: read the file with `cat` ONLY. Do NOT use `node -e`, `python3 -c`, `jq`, `awk`, or any inline interpreter — those are approval-gated and will hang unattended. Parse the JSON yourself from the cat output. Do NOT write any files.

Staging shape:
```
{
  "wiki_manifest": "...",        // shared list of existing wiki entries (for dedup)
  "candidates": [{"content_key": str, "source_name": str, "title": str, "prompt": str, "entries": null}, ...]
}
```

For EACH candidate: treat `candidate.prompt` as the extraction instruction (it carries the source content + rules + the `---SPLIT---` convention). Use the shared top-level `wiki_manifest` for dedup. Produce the wiki entry text(s): ONLY frontmatter + markdown body, each starting with `---`. If it would duplicate a manifest entry, either include `update_path: <relative_path>` in frontmatter with the FULL updated body, or skip it. Prefer skipping over low-quality extraction; do NOT invent knowledge not in the source.

Return your result by calling `tasks.handoff` with deliverableState="complete" and a `summary` that contains ONLY a fenced ```json block in this EXACT shape:
```json
{"entries": {"<content_key>": ["<full entry text>", "<another entry>"], "<content_key2>": []}}
```
Map each candidate's content_key to a LIST of its entry texts (`[]` = nothing to extract). Do NOT write the staging file — the apply step reads your handoff.

steps.name: Classifier distills taste from transcript batch
steps.promptTemplate:
You are the nightly taste CLASSIFIER. Read the staging file at `{{taste_staging_path}}`.

IMPORTANT — tool policy: read with `cat` ONLY. Do NOT use `node -e`, `python3 -c`, `jq`, `awk`, or any inline interpreter — approval-gated, will hang unattended. Parse the JSON yourself. Do NOT write any files.

Staging shape:
```
{ "principles_manifest": "...", "valid_domains": ["infra","knowledge","storytelling","design"], "transcripts": [{title, source_file, body, user_turns, assistant_turns, body_chars}, ...], "outcomes": null }
```

For each transcript (in order), decide ONE outcome:
- CANDIDATE: a new principle fingerprint not in the manifest (belief-shaped "X is Y", transfers beyond this project).
- EVIDENCE: a triangulating instance of an existing manifest principle (`matched_existing_fingerprint` MUST quote it verbatim). PREFER evidence when fingerprints converge.
- NEITHER: project-specific / log-shaped / no transferable principle. DEFAULT to NEITHER when uncertain — a polluted bundle is worse than a missed capture.

Fingerprint ≤15 words, belief-shaped, strip project-specific terms. Domain must be one of valid_domains. One transcript = at most ONE outcome.

Return via `tasks.handoff` with deliverableState="complete" and a `summary` containing ONLY a fenced ```json block:
```json
{"outcomes": [{"transcript_index": 0, "outcome": "candidate|evidence|neither", "domain": "<valid_domain>", "principle_fingerprint": "<=15 words or null", "matched_existing_fingerprint": "<verbatim or null>", "evidence_record": {"project_id": "...", "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "wiki_paths": [], "what_happened": "...", "what_triggers_principle": "..."}, "fire_signature_hints": [], "rubric_item_candidates": [], "near_miss_observed": null, "rationale": "..."}]}
```
One object per transcript, same order as `transcripts`. Do NOT write the staging file — the apply step reads your handoff.

steps.name: Apply compile entries + taste outcomes + final housekeeping
steps.promptTemplate: Run dream_cycle.apply against {{compile_staging_path}} + {{taste_staging_path}} for {{wiki_root}}. Writes the compiler agent's wiki entries, materializes the classifier's taste outcomes into skills-proposals/, then runs consolidate/reindex/codex_integration. Both apply steps fall back to an inline engine call for any candidate/transcript the agent left unprocessed, so a stalled spawn never drops the night's work.
